item.title: Books to improve yourself in your relations
item.description:
Improving Relationships - English

  • Love phobia: how to overcome your fear of commitment and abandonment - Hannah Cuppen
  • The Hold Me Tight: Seven Conversations for a Lifetime of Love - Sue Johnson

Although Hold Me Tight was originally written as a guide for Christian couples, I strongly recommend this book to all couples, regardless of their background, culture, or religion. Its insights into emotional connection, healthy communication, and secure bonding are universal and meaningful for anyone who wants to build a deeper, stronger, and more loving relationship.

item.title: Benchmark write speed
item.description:
💾 Benchmark Write Speed to a Disk Link to heading

Sometimes you need to test how fast a (network) disk can handle writes.

A common method is:

  1. Write one large file
  2. Split it into many small files
  3. Benchmark copying them using rsync or cp

Why? Some storage systems handle large sequential writes very well, but struggle with many small files (metadata overhead, IOPS limits, etc.).

item.title: Date command in bash
item.description:
Just a small example of the date command Link to heading

Show the weekday in number, always handy to know, small examples of the date command

# Display weekday
date +%u

# Display quater of the year
date +%q

#!/bin/bash
todayWeekday=$(date +%u)
todayQuater=$(date +%q)
echo -e 'The command "date +%u" gives the weekday in numbers output is:' $todayWeekday

item.title: CSS addup for alert boxes
item.description:
.Alert,
.Warning,
.Error,
.Success,
.Note {
 padding: 11px;
 margin-bottom: 24px;
 border-style: solid;
 border-width: 1px;
 border-radius: 4px;
}
.Alert p,
.Warning p,
.Error p,
.Success p,
.Note p {
 margin-top: 0;
}
.Alert p:last-child,
.Warning p:last-child,
.Error p:last-child,
.Success p:last-child,
.Note p:last-child {
 margin-bottom: 0;
}

item.title: Connect S4048 with console in Linux
item.description:
Connect serial console in Linux Link to heading

I needed to connect my Dell S4048 and my Dell Z9100-ON over a serial port in linux.

So i was trying to connect over my rs232 cable to my Dell switch. But somehow i got weird characters after connecting.

specifiations on the DELL site said

  • 115200 baud rate—set the MicroUSB console port to 9600 baud rate.
  • No parity.
  • 8 data bits.
  • 1 stop bit.
  • No flow control.

I did try minicom, and cu but with no working results Finally kermit did the trick by using a config file ~/.kermrc containing

item.title: Fetchmail OPENSSL error
item.description:
Fetchmail OpenSSL reported error Link to heading

In case you get the OpenSSL error with fetchmail you need to generate a MD5 fingerprint to validate de OpenSSL connection and add this to your ~/.fetchmailrc file. Also make sure you have a accurate version of OpenSSL installed.

#Generate MD5 
openssl s_client -connect zimap.cwi.nl:993 </dev/null 2>/dev/null | openssl x509 -fingerprint -noout -md5 -in /dev/stdin

#Add this line with your generated fingerprint to your ~/.fetchmailrc file
ssl
sslfingerprint "01:02:03:04:05:06:07:08:09:10:11:12:13:14:15:FF"

finally your config should be something like

item.title: Fetchmail OPENSSL error PART 2
item.description:
Fetchmail OpenSSL error PART 2 Link to heading

In the past fetchmail only required a md5 fingerprint of a ssl-certificate as a validation. Since ferora36 fetchmail requires a complete certificate and c_rehash files of the certificates.

This can be accomplished by the following steps

Get the domain certificate Link to heading

Save the domain certificate to some place, in my example we create a folder inside the home folder and save it there.

item.title: Best practice generate a ssh-keypair
item.description:
Introduction Link to heading

This is a short best practice how to generate a ssh-keypair at the linux commandline.

A public/private key pair is pair of mathematically related keys used in asymmetric cryptography for authentication, digital signature, or key establishment. As the name indicates, the private key is used by the owner of the key pair, is kept secret, and should be protected at all times, while the public key can be published and used be the relying party to complete the protocol or invert the operations performed with the private key.

item.title: Connect to cisco switch with SSH
item.description:
Introduction Link to heading

I got this error message when i try to login with SSH to my cisco switch.

CISCO — SSH fails to connect

Unable to negotiate with [hostname or ip] port 22: no matching key exchange method found. Their offer: diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1,diffie-hellman-group1-sha1,diffie-hellman-group14-sha1

You can solve this by using

ssh -o KexAlgorithms=diffie-hellman-group1-sha1 [hostname or ip]

item.title: Echkart Tolle Basis principes Bewustzijn
item.description:
🌿 Basisprincipes in de boeken van Eckhart Tolle Link to heading

De kern van Eckhart Tolle’s werk draait om bewustzijn, aanwezigheid en innerlijke vrijheid. Onderstaande principes vormen de ruggengraat van al zijn boeken.

1. 🌱 Leef in het huidige moment (The Power of Now) Link to heading

Het belangrijkste principe: het leven vindt alleen in het NU plaats. Niet in het verleden, niet in de toekomst. Wanneer je je aandacht terugbrengt naar het huidige moment, verdwijnt mentale ruis en ontstaat innerlijke rust.

item.title: Wellustig mors ik om mij heen
item.description:
Toon Tellegen — Wellustig mors ik om mij heen — Link to heading

Wanneer je me nu verlaat

zit ik aan een tafel, met een groot wit servet voor,
oud en blozend,
ik eet pasteien en reusachtige puddingen —
**wellustig mors ik om mij heen. **

Wanneer je nu achter mij, op je tenen, de deur uitgaat

heb ik mijn mond vol en kan niet meer.
Maar lakeien, mijn lakeien, sperren mijn kaken open
en fluisteren: denk maar aan God, onze god…

item.title: generate qrcode from pipe in linux cli
item.description:
Create a qrcode from command output Link to heading

I needed to pipe my cli output to a qrcode and generate a qrcode from it. So here a short one how to generate qrcode from pipe in linux cli.

cat ./somefile.txt | qrencode -t ansiutf8

or i manage my passowords with pass and needed to copy a password to my phone by qr

pass work/someaccount | head -n1 | tr -d \\n | qrencode -t ansiutf8

’tr’ removes the newline at the end witch is added.

item.description:

Avoid this problem by making a bootable USB with ventoy USB tool

item.title: Some tricks in bash
item.description:
Display the number of logged in users using the who command Link to heading

Keeping track of the number of users logged into a system can be incredibly useful. Here’s a concise Bash command that displays the exact count of active users

who -q | grep "users=" | sed 's/# users=//g'

For a continuous, real-time update of the user count, try piping the command to watch - it’s as easy as

item.title: YubiKey GPG Guide
item.description:
Hier is een duidelijke, complete en professionele uitleg voor het instellen van PGP op een YubiKey. Ik houd het overzichtelijk maar technisch correct.

🔐 Instellen van PGP op een YubiKey (GPG + YubiKey) Link to heading

Dit is de standaard, best-practice workflow om PGP veilig op een YubiKey te gebruiken.

1. Installeer benodigde software Link to heading

Linux Link to heading 

sudo apt install gnupg2 scdaemon pcscd yubikey-manager yubikey-personalization
sudo systemctl enable --now pcscd

macOS (via Homebrew) Link to heading 

brew install gnupg yubikey-personalization yubikey-manager
brew services start pcscd

2. Maak een offline master key Link to heading

Dit is de belangrijkste stap.

item.title: YubiKey install ykman from git
item.description:
🔧 Installing the Latest ykman (Advanced Methods) Link to heading

For advanced users, there are several ways to install the latest development version of YubiKey Manager (ykman) directly from source. Choose the method that best fits your workflow.

1. 📦 Install ykman in a Python Virtual Environment (Recommended) Link to heading

This keeps the installation isolated from your system Python and avoids conflicts.

item.title: YubiKey OATH / TOTP Guide
item.description:
🔐 YubiKey OATH / TOTP Guide Link to heading

Copying Existing TOTP Secrets into Your YubiKey Link to heading

⚠️ About This Guide Link to heading

This guide explains how to copy your existing TOTP secrets into your YubiKey.

⚠️ Warning 1 Link to heading

Use this method only if you fully understand what you are doing. TOTP secrets are extremely sensitive — if someone obtains your secret, they can generate your codes.

item.title: YubiKey SSH Guide
item.description:
SSH with YubiKey (FIDO2) Link to heading

Make sure you do the first steps of initiating your YubiKey before you will start using the SSH functionality on your yubikey.

There are still two keys generated: your private key and your public key. The private key remains important when using your YubiKey. In fact, the private key functions as a challenge key and works together with your YubiKey.

Japanese Wood Block Prints
Uchida Wood Block Printing Co., Kyoto

 

 
Uchida Wood Block Printing Co., LTD:
Japanese Wood Block Prints, Kyoto, Uchida Wood Block Printing Co., LTD, undated but ca 1950, 12mo (5 x 7 in - 12.5 x 17.9 cm), string tied pamphlet with 8 pages (four folded pages) of English text and black and white illustrations and 24 loose plates with a woodblock print tipped to each plate. The covers (front and back) of the pamphlet are illustrated with woodblock prints. The first five pages are an English text essay on "The Outline of Japanese Wood Block Print." The final three pages of the pamphlet are illustrations, with captions, which depict the carving and printing tools used in the production of wood block prints. Accompanying the pamphlet are 22 loose plates. Each plate measures 8 1/4 x 6 in (15.1 x 21.1 cm) and has a woodblock print measuring 5 3/4 x 3 7/8 in (14.6 x 9.8 cm) tipped to the page. The folio of 22 plates demonstrates, block by block, the creation of a color wood block print. The plates are numbered A1~A11 and B1-B11. The "A" plates represent the individual blocks used to create the plate. The "B" plates show the woodblock plate as it progresses to completion after the application of the successive blocks shown in the "A" plates. Plate B11 shows the completed color woodblock print after blocks A1~A11 have been applied. The folio is housed in a cardboard box with the completed woodblock print (ie B11) tipped onto the lid.

  


Pamphlet:

 

 

 

 

 

Individual Plates (Examples of the 24)
Plate Measures 8 1/4 x 6 in - 15.1 x 21.1 cm
Tipped on Woodblock Measures 5 3/4 x 3 7/8 in - 14.6 x 9.8 cm 

 
 
 
 
 

Box Cover:

Process Book of Woodblock Printing
Uchida Art Co., LTD., Kyoto

Winter Scene, Two People Walking Under Umbrella

 

 
Uchida Art Co., LTD.:
Process Book of Woodblock Printing, Kyoto, Uchida Art Co., LTD., undated but ca 1970, small 12mo (5 1/8 x 6 7/8 in - 13.2 x 17.5 cm), stiff covers with paste on title label, accordion style, plates are tipped to page at the top, the first page of text is attached as the front cover paste down. It is followed by eight fold out (attached) pages and then the final page is attached as the paste down of the back cover. This same process is then repeated going the other direction. In other words, you have 10 continuous pages on the front and back for a total there are 20 pages. The first two pages are text. The balance of the pages show the woodblock printing process with one page showing the successive plates "A1-A9" and the adjacent page (B1-9) showing the cumulative state of the woodblock print after application of the "A" woodblock. The final single plate (A9) is a colorless embossed impression which adds texture to the dress of the lady at the right and outline of the lady at the left. The book demonstrates the printing of color woodblock plate in nine steps (key block plus 8 additional plates).

This book shows a woodblock print of a winter scene and two people walking under an umbrella.

I have seen two different color covers for this book. One is a reddish brown and the other light yellow. Both have paste on title labels.

Earlier, Uchida used this same basic technique ("A" and "B" plates), but loose format, to demonstrate the woodblock printing process.

 

Completed Woodblock Print

Ten pages in each direction.


 All Pages

 

 

 

 

 

 

 
 
 

 

 
 (A9 is a colorless embossed impression for the dress of the lady at the right and 
 the outline of the lady at the left

Process Book of Woodblock Printing
Uchida Art Co., LTD., Kyoto

Two Children Playing

 

 
Uchida Art Co., LTD.:
Process Book of Woodblock Printing, Kyoto, Uchida Art Co., LTD., undated but ca 1970, small 12mo (5 1/8 x 6 7/8 in - 13.2 x 17.5 cm), light gold stiff covers with paste on title label, accordion style, plates are tipped to page at the top, the first page of text is attached as the front cover paste down. It is followed by eight fold out (attached) pages and then the final page is attached as the paste down of the back cover. This same process is then repeated going the other direction. In other words, you have 11 continuous pages on the front and back for a total there are 22 pages. The first two pages are text. The balance of the pages show the woodblock printing process with one page showing the successive plates "A1-A11" and the adjacent page (B1-11) showing the cumulative state of the woodblock print after application of the "A" woodblock. This book shows a woodblock print of two children playing. The book demonstrates the printing of a color woodblock plate in ten steps (key block plus 9 additional plates).

This book shows a woodblock print of two children playing.

I have seen two different color covers for this book. One is a reddish brown and the other light yellow. Both have paste on title labels.

Earlier, Uchida used this same basic technique ("A" and "B" plates), but loose format, to demonstrate the woodblock printing process.

 

Completed Woodblock Print

Two Pages of Test and Twenty two prints (10 front to back and 12 back to front). .


 All Pages

item.title: Readme
item.description:
🌈 Benjamin’s Life Link to heading

Hey there! 🌞 Link to heading

I’m Benjamin Italiaander — and welcome to my cheerful little corner of the world! 💛✨
This is where I collect and share all the things that make my days brighter and my heart lighter! 🌸

Here you’ll find:

  • 🍲 Delicious food recipes I love to make (and of course, eat!)
  • 📚 My favorite books that inspire me, surprise me, or simply make me smile
  • 💬 Uplifting quotes & thoughts that fill me with calm and positivity
  • 🎸 Fun music tabs I enjoy playing and learning
  • 💻 My handy Linux notes — because even tech can bring a little joy!

This is my happy place — a colorful mix of creativity, curiosity, and sunshine vibes! ☀️💫
Grab a cup of tea, take a peek around, and maybe you’ll find a spark of happiness too! 🌻💖

item.title: 0001 Two Gun Crowley
item.description:
Two Gun Crowley — The “Cop Killer” Link to heading

Before he was caught, Francis “Two Gun” Crowley left this note on a piece of paper:

“To whom it may concern: Under my coat is a weary heart — but a kind one, one that would do nobody any harm.”

Crowley was later sentenced to death by the electric chair. When he arrived at the death house in Sing Sing Prison, the guards told him what awaited him.

item.title: 0002 We Dread Conemnation
item.description:
Hans Selye on Human Nature Link to heading

Hans Selye, a renowned psychologist, once said:

“As much as we thirst for approval, we dread condemnation.”

Translated to Dutch

“Wij verlangen net zo veel naar goedkeuring als we veroordeling vrezen.”

item.title: 0003 Abraham Lincoln
item.description:
Abraham Lincoln on Understanding Others Link to heading

Abraham Lincoln once said:

“Don’t criticize them; they are just what we would be under similar circumstances.”

Translated to Dutch

“bekritiseer hen niet; zij doen precies wat wij zouden doen onder vergelijkbare omstandigheden.”

item.title: 0004 Father Forgets
item.description:
Father Forgets Link to heading

Father Forgets by W. Livingston Larned was first published in 1927. It originally appeared as an editorial in the People’s Home Journal, and it quickly became widely reprinted in newspapers, magazines, and anthologies — especially after Dale Carnegie featured it in How to Win Friends and Influence People (1936).

Listen, son: I am saying this as you lie asleep, one little paw crumpled under your cheek and the blond curls stickily wet on your damp forehead. I have stolen into your room alone. Just a few minutes ago, as I sat reading my paper in the library, a stifling wave of remorse swept over me. Guiltily I came to your bedside.

item.title: 0005 Nourishment For My Self Esteem
item.description:
Alfred Lunt on Emotional Nourishment Link to heading

Alfred Lunt (1892 – 1977), an acclaimed American stage actor known for his refined performances and partnership with his wife, Lynn Fontanne, once said:

“There is nothing I need so much as nourishment for my self-esteem.”

Translated to Dutch

“Er is niets dat ik zo nodig heb als voedsel voor mijn gevoel van eigenwaarde.”

This quote, often cited in collections of theatre and motivational sayings, reflects Lunt’s deep awareness of the emotional vulnerability behind artistic expression. It’s believed to have been said during the mid-20th century — around the 1940s – 1950s — when Lunt and Fontanne were among Broadway’s most celebrated duos.

item.title: 0006 Hart Verstand
item.description:
Over Hart en Verstand Link to heading

Het een komt uit het hart, het ander komt uit het berekenende verstand.

Het een is onzelfzuchtig, het ander egoïstisch.

Het een wordt algemeen bewonderd, het ander wordt door iedereen afgekeurd.

item.title: 0007 Trust And Deception
item.description:
Álvaro Obregón on Trust and Deception Link to heading

Álvaro Obregón (1880 – 1928), Mexicaans generaal, revolutionair leider en later president van Mexico, once said:

“Do not fear the enemies who attack you, fear the friends who flatter you.”

“Vrees niet de vijanden die U aanvallen, vrees de vrienden die U vleien.”

Context / Notes:

  • Year: ca. 1920s
  • Obregón was a key figure in the Mexican Revolution and known for his sharp political insight.
  • This quote reflects his pragmatic and wary view of loyalty, drawn from years of political and military struggle.

item.title: 001 Later Als Ik Dood Ben
item.description:
Later als ik dood ben Link to heading

Intro Link to heading

item.title: 001 Principes
item.description:
Murphyologische principes Link to heading

De wet van Murphy is de nadere uitwerking van en het logische vervolg op de algemene entropieleer.

Entropieleer is alles dat beweegt richting chaos, het gaat over het universum dat sowieso al richting chaos beweegt waardoor dingen dus ook vanzelf mis gaan.

  • Je kamer wordt vanzelf rommelig, maar hij wordt nooit vanzelf netjes.
  • Warme koffie wordt vanzelf koud, maar koude koffie wordt niet vanzelf warm.
  • Een ijsblokje smelt vanzelf, maar smeltwater vormt niet spontaan weer een ijsblokje.

De wet van Murphy Link to heading

Wanneer iets mis kán gaan, gáát het ook mis. Met de uitvinding van de computer probeerde de mens voor het eerst levenloze materie een zekere mate van intelligentie mee te geven. Een fatale beslissing. Sinds de introductie van de computer kunnen wij de wet van Murphy uitbreiden met de volgende digitale afleidingen:

item.title: 008 We Never Rise Above The Opinion Of Ourselves
item.description:
People will never rise above their opinion of themselves

Peter Sage

Peter Sage is making a mindset statement about how your self-image sets the ceiling for your life. Here’s what he means, in simple terms:

⭐ The core idea Link to heading

You cannot consistently behave, choose, or grow in ways that contradict your belief about who you are. If you think you are “not smart,” “not lovable,” “not capable,” or “not worthy,” your life will unconsciously adjust downward to match that belief.

item.title: 009 How To Win Riends
item.description:
How to Win Friends & Influence People — Quotes Link to heading

Once I did bad and that I heard ever. Twice I did good, but that I heard never.

It isn’t what you have or who you are or where you are or what you are doing that makes you happy or unhappy.
It is what you think about it.

Don’t be afraid of enemies who attack you.
Be afraid of the friends who flatter you.

item.title: Bash Otp Generator
item.description:
Create an OTP MFA Tool in the Bash Terminal Link to heading

I was tired of using Google Authenticator, so I found a simple way to generate my own OTP (One-Time Password) codes directly in Bash.
Just replace the example string below (JHJH...) with your own secret key.

/usr/bin/oathtool -b --totp 'JHJHJHJHJHJHJHJHJHJHJHJHJ'

If you want to copy the token directly to your clipboard, just use xclip:

item.title: Bearos Start Point
item.description:
The first basics of Bareos Link to heading

Main components of Bareos Link to heading

Bareos is made up of the following major components or services:

  • Bareos Director Service
  • Console service
  • Bareos File deamon
  • Bareos Storage Deamon
  • Bareos Monitor service

Bareos Director Service Link to heading

The Director is the central control program for all the other daemons. It schedules and supervises all the backup, restore, verify and archive operations. The system administrator uses the Bareos Director to schedule backups and to recover files. The Director runs as a daemon (or service) in the background.

item.title: Bye Bye Screen Hello Zellij
item.description:
zellij is a great alternative for screen! Link to heading

I just came across a really nice tool zellij as a replacement of the tmux or screen terminal multiplexing applications. I instantly became a fan! Link to heading

zellij can be found here it’s not in all repository’s yet, but easy to build from source or download the binary and copy it into your /sbin folder

item.title: Chmod Ssh Files
item.description:
chmod ssh files Link to heading

  • SSH folder - ~/.ssh 700 drwx——
  • Public key - ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub 644 -rw-r–r–
  • Private key - ~/.ssh/id_rsa 600 -rw——-
  • Authorized Keys - ~/.ssh/authorized_keys 600 -rw——-
  • Config - ~/.ssh/config 600 -rw——-

home folder Link to heading

chmod g-w,o-w ~ Home folder ~ 755 at most drwxr-xr-x at most

item.title: Copy And Paste In Cli
item.description:
Copying and pasting in the Linux terminal is quite easy with xclip. Link to heading

simple bash command Link to heading 

# This command copys
xclip -selection c

making it in a function Link to heading

I added in my .bashrc file a function because i keep forgetting it.

# call this function by just open copy-to-clipboard

copy-to-clipboard(){
 xclip -selection c
 }

#for example:
cat some-file.txt | copy-to-clipboard

item.title: Diff
item.description:
show the difference between two files Link to heading

Colomed output

diff -y --side-by-side file_one.txt file_two.txt

Colomed output filtered

diff -y --side-by-side --suppress-common-lines file_one.txt file_two.txt

diff --side-by-side file_one.txt file_two.txt

diff --side-by-side --suppress-common-lines file_one.txt file_two.txt

item.title: En Compassion Determination
item.description:
Three Things That Always Help Me Link to heading

There’s three things that I think always help me.

1. Compassion for Other People Link to heading

First of all, have compassion for other people. Treat people like you would like to be treated. Treat people nicely on your way up. You might meet them on the way down.

It’s nice to be important, but it’s also more important to be nice.

item.title: En Deal With Situations
item.description:
🌿 The Eckhart Tolle Formula for Dealing with Difficult Situations Link to heading

Eckhart Tolle explains that in every uncomfortable or challenging situation, you ultimately have three conscious choices. These choices bring clarity, calmness, and responsibility back to yourself.

1. ✔️ Acceptance Link to heading

Question: Can I accept the situation exactly as it is? Sometimes a situation cannot be changed immediately. By fully accepting what is happening, you release inner resistance and create space for peace and clarity.

item.title: En How To Get Old
item.description:
🌿 The Formula for Growing Old Happily and Healthily Link to heading

Growing old in a happy and healthy way is not a mystery. It comes down to a simple but powerful formula. This formula helps keep both your body and mind in balance.

1. 😴 Getting Enough Sleep Link to heading

A good night’s sleep is the foundation of recovery. While you sleep, your body repairs itself, processes emotions, and recharges your energy. Recommended: 8–10 hours of sleep per night, depending on your age and what your body needs.

item.title: En How To Say No
item.description:
How to say no the right way Link to heading

Here’s where the mistake usually happens: We feel, “I don’t want to disappoint them. I don’t want them to dislike me.” And then we respond with something like: “Oh wow, that sounds great, I’d love to… but I can’t, I have a full day of meetings.”

The problem is the word “but.” “But” has a way of erasing everything that came before it. If you say, “I love you, but…” whatever comes after makes the first part feel less genuine.

item.title: En Install All Dependencies
item.description:
date: 2025-10-01T12:30:21+01:00 title: “Before installing YubiKey’s ykman software” draft: false description: “1. Install the first dependicies on your system”

Before you install ykman Link to heading

You will need to have the following onboard

apt install gnupg2 scdaemon pcscd pip pipx
  • GnuPG — The Universal Crypto Engine
  • scdaemon - Smartcard daemon for the GnuPG system
  • pcscd - PC/SC Smart Card Daemon
  • pipx - execute binaries from Python packages in isolated environments

item.title: En Modern Love
item.description:
Modern Love and Emotional Defensiveness Link to heading

People nowadays aren’t capable of loving. They’re too protective. They’re too defensive. They don’t want to feel hurt because they don’t want to be hurt. They don’t want to give so much of themselves. They don’t want to be generous with their spirit. They want to protect themselves.

So, at the slightest kind of conflict with with their partner, they retreat and they get into their shell. People are more defensive. They’re more locked inside themselves. They’re more locked inside their phone.

item.title: En Sitting In The Mud
item.description:
Friendship, Loyalty, and Showing Up Link to heading

And I remember telling one of my close friends, I said, we we were riding bikes somewhere and I just out of the blue, I don’t know why it hit me, I I turned to him and I said, you know, you’re one of those friends that if I was really in the I would call you.

item.title: En The Inner Ceiling
item.description:
The Glass Ceiling in Your Mind Link to heading

Why Do Intelligent People Procrastinate? Link to heading

Why is it that intelligent people procrastinate? Why is it that people who are so self-motivated often self-sabotage? And why is it that no matter how many new business opportunities we get or new skills we try to learn, many of us simply find different ways of earning the same amount of money or achieving the same level of success?

item.title: En Yubikey Firts Time Use
item.description:
YubiKey bare minimum Link to heading

A short Getting Started Using the Linux CLI Link to heading

Step 1 till step 8 are the absolute bare minimum you should complete before you start using your YubiKey. If you skip these steps, there is nothing truly secure about using your YubiKey.

Hardware tokens are a powerful tool and can raise your security to a very high level—but only when used correctly. Be aware that using hardware tokens incorrectly will not result in a more secure user environment.

item.title: Extract Publickey From Privatekey Openssl
item.description:
Optionally, the rsa public key can be extracted from the private key Link to heading 

openssl rsa -in domain.tld.key -pubout -out doamin.tld.pubkey
openssl rsa -in domain.tld.pubkey -pubin -noout -text

Extracting the First Column Link to heading

item.title: Find Exclude Hidden Files
item.description:
Find files and igonre hidden (.*) files Link to heading 

find /home/user ! -path '*/.*' -type f

you can replace /home/user to your desired folder. you can change the type f (file) or d (directory)

item.title: Firefox Already Running
item.description:
firefox already running Link to heading

If you try to open firefox and get the message firefox already running, you may try this script

#!/bin/bash
files=`find ~/.mozilla -name "*lock"`
for file in `echo $files`
do
 echo "removing $file..."
 rm "$file"
done

item.title: First Tape Commands In Linux
item.description:
Manage tape autoloader drives and libraries for Unix/Linux CLI Link to heading

First steps of managing tapes in linux is using the MT and MTX command.

Tools used in this article are

  • lsscsi (list SCSI devices (or hosts), list NVMe devices)
  • mt (control magnetic tape drive operation from mt-st package) used to control the tape drive like tape movements, ejection, and drive settings.
  • mtx (control SCSI media changer devices) used to control tape libraries (moving cartridges from slots to drives, etc)
  • tar (an archiving utility)
  • ltfs (Linear Tape File System)
  • sg3-utils (The sg3_utils package contains utilities that send SCSI commands to devices)
  • dd (convert and copy a file)

Warning! First time loading a LTO9 tape it automatically initiates tape calibration sometimes taking up to 2 hours Link to heading

When LTO9 media is first loaded into the LTO9 drive, the drive automatically initiates tape calibration, which includes initialization and characterization of the cartridge.

Just a reminder how to do stuff

A simple way to make a expression devide

\frac {(4+b)} {(b-2)}

$$\frac {(4+b)} {(b-2)}$$

\begin{align*} & a_{ijk} = 2 \ &(because ||V_1-V_2|| = \max_{i \in [d]}|V^i_1 - V^i_2|) \end{align*}

Now align this to the left $$\left{( \frac {(4+b)} {(b-2)})}\left$$

item.title: Keyboard Not Working On Fujitsu Lifebook On Fedora
item.description:
title: “Keyboard not working on Fujitsu Lifebook on Fedora 36” author:

  • Benjamin Italiaander date: 2022-08-19 keywords:
    • Fujitsu U7410
    • Fedora
    • keyboard
    • fujitsu lifebook u7410
    • lifebook
    • U7410

So the after installing Fedora Linux 36 on my fujitsu lifebook u7410 my keyboard was not working anymore. I fixed this by editing grub.

Just add at the bottom of /etc/default/grub the following line

GRUB_CMDLINE_LINUX_DEFAULT="quiet splash i8042.reset i8042.nomux i8042.nopnp i8042.noloop"

After editing the grub file update the grubfile

item.title: Nl Anticiperend Plannen
item.description:
Anticiperend plannen Link to heading

Wanneer je hoofd overloopt van stressvolle gedachten over hoe mensen zich voelen
of hoe ze elkaar bejegenen, ben je aan het anticiperen.

Daardoor kom je in een staat van hyperalertheid terecht.

Dit gebeurt nog vóórdat een situatie daadwerkelijk plaatsvindt.

item.title: Nl Compassion Determination
item.description:
Drie Dingen Die Altijd Helpen Link to heading

Er zijn drie dingen waarvan ik denk dat ze altijd helpen.

1. Compassie voor Andere Mensen Link to heading

Allereerst: heb compassie voor andere mensen. Behandel mensen zoals jij zelf behandeld wilt worden. Behandel mensen vriendelijk terwijl je omhoog klimt — je kunt ze zomaar weer tegenkomen als je omlaag gaat.

Het is fijn om belangrijk te zijn, maar het is nóg belangrijker om aardig te zijn.

item.title: Nl Deal With Situations
item.description:
🌿 De Eckhart Tolle-Formule voor Omgaan met Ongemakkelijke Situaties Link to heading

Eckhart Tolle beschrijft dat je in elke lastige of ongemakkelijke situatie uiteindelijk drie bewuste keuzes hebt. Deze keuzes brengen rust, helderheid en verantwoordelijkheid terug naar jouzelf.

1. ✔️ Accepteren Link to heading

Vraag: Kan ik de situatie accepteren zoals die nu is? Soms kun je een situatie niet direct veranderen. Door volledig te accepteren wat er gebeurt, laat je innerlijke weerstand los en ontstaat er ruimte voor rust en helderheid.

item.title: Nl Esther Perel Wat Wij Zoeken
item.description:
1. De paradox binnen een veilige relatie Link to heading

Volgens Esther Perel is liefde niet hetzelfde als verlangen — en juist in langdurige relaties botsen verbondenheid en spanning vaak met elkaar.

Ze zegt eigenlijk: we willen twee dingen die elkaar soms tegenwerken.

  1. Veiligheid & geliefd zijn
  2. Spanning & levendigheid

En daar begint de frictie.

💛 Geliefd zijn Link to heading

Volgens Perel draait “geliefd zijn” om:

item.title: Nl Hata
item.description:
HATA – Snelle Emotie-Check Link to heading

Gebaseerd op Running on Empty van Jonice Webb

1. H — Herken Link to heading

Sta even stil en vraag jezelf:

  • Wat voel ik nu precies?
  • Wat merk ik in mijn lichaam?
  • Wat is de eerste emotie die opkomt?

Voorbeelden:

  • spanning in borst
  • irritatie
  • leeg / vlak
  • hartslag omhoog
  • druk in hoofd

2. A — Accepteer Link to heading

Laat het gevoel er zijn zonder oordeel.

item.title: Nl How To Get Old
item.description:
🌿 De Formule voor Gelukkig en Gezond Oud Worden Link to heading

Gelukkig en gezond oud worden is geen mysterie. Het bestaat uit een eenvoudige, maar krachtige formule. Deze formule helpt je zowel lichamelijk als mentaal in balans te blijven.

1. 😴 Voldoende slapen Link to heading

Een goede nachtrust is de basis van herstel. Tijdens het slapen herstelt je lichaam, verwerk je emoties en laadt je energie opnieuw op. Aanbevolen: 8–10 uur slaap per nacht, afhankelijk van je leeftijd en wat je lichaam aangeeft.

item.title: Nl How To Say No
item.description:
Hoe zeg je correct nee Link to heading

Bij nee zeggen gaat het meestal alsvolgt mis:: We denken “Ik wil hen niet teleurstellen. Ik wil niet dat ze me niet meer leuk vinden.” En dan zeggen we iets als: “Oh wauw, dat klinkt geweldig, ik zou heel graag willen… maar ik kan niet, ik heb de hele dag meetings.”

Het probleem is het woord “maar.” “Maar” heeft de neiging alles ervoor te laten verdwijnen. Als je zegt: “Ik hou van je, maar…” dan voelt alles wat je daarvoor zei ineens minder oprecht.

item.title: Nl Modern Love
item.description:
Moderne Liefde en Emotionele Verdediging Link to heading

Mensen zijn tegenwoordig niet meer in staat om lief te hebben. Ze zijn te beschermend. Ze zijn te defensief.

Ze willen geen pijn voelen omdat ze niet gekwetst willen worden. Ze willen niet te veel van zichzelf geven. Ze willen niet vrijgevig zijn met hun geest, met hun hart. Ze willen zichzelf beschermen.

Dus bij het minste of geringste conflict met hun partner trekken ze zich terug en kruipen ze in hun schulp. Mensen zijn defensiever. Ze zitten meer opgesloten in zichzelf. Ze zitten meer opgesloten in hun telefoon.

item.title: Nl Selfcare Janice W
item.description:
🧩 Zelfzorg-Checklist (Dagelijks) Link to heading

Gebaseerd op inzichten uit Running on Empty

🌿 Basis lichamelijke zorg Link to heading

  • Heb ik genoeg water gedronken vandaag?
  • Heb ik op tijd gegeten (niet te weinig, niet te veel)?
  • Heb ik voeding gekozen die mij goed doet?
  • Heb ik pauzes genomen wanneer mijn lichaam moe werd?
  • Heb ik vandaag minimaal 10 minuten bewogen?

🧘‍♂️ Rust & herstel Link to heading

  • Heb ik vandaag momenten van echte rust gehad (zonder scherm)?
  • Heb ik mijn ademhaling bewust even rustig gemaakt?
  • Heb ik vandaag iets gedaan dat mijn stress verlaagt?
  • Heb ik vroeg genoeg aangegeven dat ik overprikkeld raakte?
  • Heb ik mijn avond rustig afgesloten?

💛 Emotionele check-in Link to heading

  • Heb ik vandaag stilgestaan bij wat ik voel?
  • Heb ik dat gevoel toegestaan zonder oordeel?
  • Heb ik 1–2 minuten genomen om een gevoel er gewoon te laten zijn?
  • Heb ik iets gedaan dat mijn emotie ondersteunt (i.p.v. onderdrukt)?

🔄 Grenzen & balans Link to heading

  • Heb ik “nee” gezegd waar dat nodig was?
  • Heb ik iets niet gedaan waar ik eigenlijk geen energie voor had?
  • Heb ik mensen niet boven mezelf gezet ten koste van mijn eigen welzijn?
  • Heb ik hulp gevraagd als iets te zwaar voelde?

🌼 Vriendelijkheid naar mezelf Link to heading

  • Heb ik vandaag één kleine vriendelijke actie voor mezelf gedaan?
  • Heb ik mezelf niet gekleineerd of streng benaderd?
  • Heb ik mezelf behandeld zoals ik een vriend(in) zou behandelen?

item.title: Nl Sitting In The Mud
item.description:
Vriendschap, Loyaliteit en de Eer om Er Te Zijn Link to heading

Ik herinner me dat ik ooit tegen een van mijn beste vrienden zei—we waren ergens aan het fietsen, het leven voelde licht, en uit het niets, zonder dat ik wist waarom, keek ik hem aan en zei:

“Je bent zo’n vriend… als ik ooit echt in de ellende zou zitten, dan zou ik jóu bellen.”

item.title: Nl The Inner Ceiling
item.description:
Waarom Stellen Intelligente Mensen Dingen Uit? Link to heading

Waarom stellen intelligente mensen dingen uit? Waarom saboteren mensen die zo gemotiveerd zijn vaak zichzelf? En waarom is het zo dat, hoeveel nieuwe kansen of vaardigheden we ook krijgen, veel van ons toch eindigen met ongeveer hetzelfde inkomen of hetzelfde niveau van succes?

Het voelt vaak alsof er een glazen plafond boven ons hoofd hangt waar we maar niet doorheen breken.

item.title: Nl Whats Behind The Emotions
item.description:
echte gevoelens versus afweerreacties Link to heading

Het verschil tussen echte gevoelens versus afweerreacties die ontstaan vanuit een oude pijn.

🌱 1. Basisemoties (Echte Gevoelens) Link to heading

Dit zijn de oorspronkelijke, zuivere emoties die bij een kind horen en die in het heden opnieuw getriggerd kunnen worden:

✔️ Verdriet Link to heading

Het gevoel van gemis, pijn of verlatenheid.

item.title: Nl Yubikey Ingebruikname
item.description:
YubiKey basisconfiguratie Link to heading

Een korte introductie voor gebruik via de Linux CLI Link to heading

Stap 1 t/m stap 8 zijn het absolute minimum dat je moet uitvoeren voordat je je YubiKey in gebruik neemt. Als je deze stappen overslaat, is er niets echt veilig aan het gebruik van je YubiKey.

Hardware-tokens zijn een krachtig hulpmiddel en kunnen je beveiliging naar een zeer hoog niveau tillen — maar alleen wanneer je ze correct gebruikt. Wees je ervan bewust dat hardware-tokens verkeerd gebruiken níét resulteert in een veiliger gebruikersomgeving.

item.title: Nl Yubikey Linux Gui Login
item.description:
Hier is een duidelijke en complete handleiding om op Ubuntu in te loggen met je YubiKey (PAM + FIDO2 / WebAuthn). Dit werkt voor:

  • Ubuntu desktop login (GDM)
  • sudo
  • su
  • TTY login

Ik geef zowel de snelle versie als de uitgebreide versie.

✅ Korte versie (TL;DR) Link to heading

  1. Installeer FIDO2-tools:
sudo apt install libpam-u2f pamu2fcfg
  1. Maak de config-map aan:
mkdir -p ~/.config/Yubico
  1. Registreer je YubiKey:
pamu2fcfg > ~/.config/Yubico/u2f_keys

(raak de YubiKey aan)

item.title: Openpgp Gnupg Cheat Sheet
item.description:
A small list with som important commands i use with GnuPG Link to heading

YubiKey and gnuPG OpenPGP or gpg Link to heading

Just start with

# open the gpg with your yubikey
gpg --card-edit

# Now enter the admin command
gpg/card> admin

# Now enter Help and you see all possible commands
gpg/card> help
quit quit this menu
admin show admin commands
help show this help
list list all available data
name change card holder's name
url change URL to retrieve key
fetch fetch the key specified in the card URL
login change the login name
lang change the language preferences
salutation change card holder's salutation
cafpr change a CA fingerprint
forcesig toggle the signature force PIN flag
generate generate new keys
passwd menu to change or unblock the PIN
verify verify the PIN and list all data
unblock unblock the PIN using a Reset Code
factory-reset destroy all keys and data
kdf-setup setup KDF for PIN authentication
key-attr change the key attribute

List all keys including keygrip or keyidentity on a yubiKey

item.title: Outgoing Connections
item.description:
#How to filter out outgoing connections on the linux cli This is a small example how

Filter outoutgoing TCP connections

# Show ougoing TCP connections
netstat -atn | tr -s ' '| cut -f5 -d ' ' | grep -v '127.0.0.1'

Filter out going UDP connections

# Show outgoing UDP connections
netstat -aun | tr -s ' '| cut -f5 -d ' ' | grep -v '127.0.0.1'

item.title: Quotations From Jana
item.description:
De uitdrukking “beminnelijke deugd der inschikkelijkheid” klinkt heel ouderwets en plechtig. Hier is wat het betekent in normaal, modern taalgebruik:

📘 Betekenis Link to heading

  • Beminnelijke deugd → een aantrekkelijke, sympathieke eigenschap
  • Inschikkelijkheid → meegaandheid, soepel zijn, bereid zijn je aan te passen aan anderen

👉 Samengevat Link to heading

“De beminnelijke deugd der inschikkelijkheid” betekent: de mooie en sympathieke eigenschap dat iemand gemakkelijk met anderen meebeweegt en niet moeilijk doet.”

Reading markDown files in the Linux commandline Link to heading

One handy tool i came across is Glow well maintained and easy to use

frogmouth is a simple MD vieuwer in the CLI build with textual(https://github.com/Textualize/textual )

Markdown lint tool fr the MD purists a check tool for you md files. Not wel maintained.

item.title: Remove Blank Spaces
item.description:
Remove blankspace from output Link to heading

If you have a pattern like this in a file: abcd efdg ijklm sfgsdf sdgf gfgf gdfg dgdffdg

and you like to convert it in seperat the words per line The most easiest way of removing whitespace from a pattern like

cat ./somefile.txt | xargs -n 1

I’m Benjamin Italiaander — and welcome to my cheerful little corner of the world! 💛✨

item.title: Some Instruction Aboutssl I Found Online
item.description:
i found this useful instruction here Thank you for sharing Igor Soarez

How to setup your own CA with OpenSSL Link to heading

For educational reasons I’ve decided to create my own CA. Here is what I learned.

First things first Link to heading

Lets get some context first.

Public Key Cryptography Link to heading

AKA asymmetric cryptography solves the problem of two entities communicating securely without ever exchanging a common key, by using two related keys, one private, one public.

item.title: SSH config file
item.description:
ssh config client config file Link to heading

File is located at Link to heading 

~/.ssh/config

Example 1 Link to heading

This is a regular config

Host server.tld
 User username
 IdentityFile /home/username/.ssh/id_rsa
 ForwardAgent yes

The ForwardAgent option allows you to use your private, local SSH key remotely without worrying about leaving confidential data on the server you’re working with. It’s built into ssh, and is easy to set up and use.

item.title: Ssl Certificate Signing Request Best Practices
item.description:
Certificate Signing Request Best Practices Using OpenSSL Link to heading

A Certificate Signing Request (CSR) can be generated with OpenSSL, during the creation of a CSR you generate two files, A private key (.key file) and a certificate signing request (.csr file)

Best way of generating a CSR is by creating a request configuration file where you add all the SSL info. In this case i call the config file request.cnf.

item.title: Ssl Checks
item.description:
Regular ssl checks Link to heading

Check a Certificate Signing Request (CSR)

openssl req -text -noout -verify -in CSR.csr

Check a private key

openssl rsa -in privateKey.key -check

Check a certificate

openssl x509 -in certificate.crt -text -noout

Check a PKCS#12 file (.pfx or .p12)

openssl pkcs12 -info -in keyStore.p12

Check if certificates are a pair Link to heading

You can check if a crt and a key file are a match with a md5 check, if the output of de md5 is the same you have a matching ssl-pair

item.title: Ssl Rehash And Filepermissions
item.description:
Link to heading 

#!/bin/bash -

uid=$1
gid=$2
uid=${uid:="nginx"}
gid=${gid:=$uid}

/bin/rm *.0

ln -s ca-bundle.crt `openssl x509 -hash -noout -in ca-bundle.crt`.0

cat asterisk_cwi_nl.crt > ssl-bundle.crt
echo >> ssl-bundle.crt
cat ca-bundle.crt >> ssl-bundle.crt
ln -s ssl-bundle.crt `openssl x509 -hash -noout -in ssl-bundle.crt`.0

if [ -f asterisk_project_cwi_nl.crt ]; then
 cat asterisk_project_cwi_nl.crt > project-bundle.crt
 echo >> project-bundle.crt
 cat ca-bundle.crt >> project-bundle.crt
 ln -s project-bundle.crt `openssl x509 -hash -noout -in project-bundle.crt`.0
fi

chmod 0444 *.crt
chmod 0400 *.key
chown $uid:$gid *.{crt,key}

Get local port for display Link to heading

--------------------------

Find local port

# virsh domdisplay --domain debian12 
spice://127.0.0.1:5900
